description_long: This is a classic cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top, featuring a weathered and rusted exterior. The main body is vertically ribbed and painted a dark, almost black color, with significant patches of rust and wear, especially around the base and outlets. The top dome is a lighter, off-white color, also showing signs of rust and aging. There are three prominent hose connection outlets: two on the sides and one facing forward, each with a round cap and visible bolt details. The operating nut is present at the very top of the dome, and the hydrant is mounted on a square base. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant appears robust and heavy, typical for urban street installations.
description: A weathered, cylindrical fire hydrant with a ribbed dark body, off-white domed top, and three hose outlets with caps.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main hose outlet and cap, with the operating nut visible on top and the ribbed body clearly displayed.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the hydrant, with the dome and ribbed body visible, and side outlets partially obscured.
description_view_2: Side view focusing on one of the side hose outlets and its cap, with the dome and ribbed body in profile.
description_view_3: Angled view showing both side outlets and caps, as well as the front outlet, providing a comprehensive look at all connection points.
